rationale_for_baseline_tier: A high-risk suspected SCC with a palpable regional node and active bleeding on apixaban needs urgent same-day in-person haemostasis and an urgent fast-track surgical/oncology referral — a telehealth provider cannot cauterise or biopsy. Escalate to T4 if bleeding is uncontrolled.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Bandage soaked and blood dripping continuously despite 20 minutes of firm pressure and elevation (on apixaban)
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Transfer to ED for surgical haemostasis
escalation_edges.rationale: Uncontrolled haemorrhage on anticoagulation needs procedural haemostasis.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the bandage soaks through and keeps dripping despite 20 minutes of firm pressure and elevation, arrange transfer to the emergency department.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: High-risk suspected SCC with a regional node
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Urgent in-person assessment today + fast-track surgical/oncology referral
escalation_edges.communication_template: This lump needs urgent in-person assessment today for the bleeding, and an urgent specialist appointment to remove and test it and check the armpit lump.

contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower threshold for the bleeding safety-net; coordinate perioperative anticoagulation with the team; do not stop apixaban unilaterally.
Give staff clear haemostasis/monitoring instructions and ensure the fast-track referral proceeds.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: This needs urgent in-person care today for the bleeding and an urgent specialist appointment to remove and test the lump and check the armpit lump. Keep the hand elevated and the dressing on; staff should press firmly and go to ED if it soaks through.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Same-day in-person (GP/nursing) haemostasis + urgent fast-track surgical/oncology referral; ED for uncontrolled bleeding
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: Attend/transfer to ED for uncontrolled bleeding; arrange the urgent referral as soon as possible.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Document a rapidly growing bleeding indurated keratotic hand nodule (suspected SCC) with a palpable axillary node, apixaban use, haemostasis measures, and an urgent fast-track surgical/oncology referral.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Reassuring as benign/traumatic with no urgent excision/staging or bleeding management.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: An invasive SCC with nodal spread is left untreated and progresses/metastasises, and the anticoagulated bleeding is unmanaged.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Only routine referral without urgent same-day bleeding management or fast-tracking the excision/staging.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Ambulance is appropriate if bleeding is uncontrolled; otherwise same-day in-person care (T3-T4) suffices.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Cannot cauterise, palpate induration/nodes, or biopsy remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat as a high-risk suspected SCC: urgent in-person haemostasis + fast-track excision/staging referral, and an ED safety-net for uncontrolled bleeding.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: False reassurance/routine referral, or under-managing the anticoagulated bleeding.
